events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse image: digital micrometer reading 27.07 mm. Rotor hat casting min TH 28.0 mm. Measurement at pad contact area, four points averaged. Measured thickness 27.07 mm is below minimum thickness 28.0 mm. Rotor is worn, must fail, high/critical risk? Since below discard thickness, rotor is unsafe, must replace. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? For brake rotor below minimum thickness, risk high. Could be critical because structural failure. But typical benchmark likely high. Component conditions: worn, maybe degraded/failed. Need include worn. value 27.07. percent_complete maybe 100? Stage tested/inspected. findings: measured rotor thickness 27.07 mm below manufacturer minimum 28.0 mm. actions: replace front rotor, inspect pads, machine/replace as necessary, verify runout etc. rationale. references: OEM minimum thickness specification. Need fill all fields. sound_source etc not relevant: empty string or null? Schema says string for sound_source, numbers for event_time, rate. Use defaults: sound_source: "", event_time: 0.0, rate: 0.0. order empty list, workable empty list. confidence 1.0. Ensure valid JSON.

    "Front brake rotor outboard pad-contact thickness measures 27.07 mm",
    "Manufacturer minimum thickness (MIN TH) is 28.0 mm",
    "Rotor is below discard specification and must not remain in service"

  "actions": [
    "Replace the front brake rotor",
    "Inspect brake pads and hardware; replace if worn or damaged",

    "Re-measure new rotor and record final thickness"

  "rationale": "The measured rotor thickness of 27.07 mm is less than the stamped minimum thickness of 28.0 mm, so the rotor is worn beyond the OEM service limit and must be replaced.",

}
